result: WALL-CLOCK validation of the E5 flop win. Multifrontal vs dense Gauss solve, d 105->889: MEASURED speedup 5.9x->255x, grows exactly as O(d*w^3) vs O(d^3) predicts (CONFIRMS direction) but ~5x BELOW the analytic flop count (18x-1270x) due to per-clique small-Vec allocation overhead. At d=889: 85us vs 21.8ms. Flop count is the CEILING a contiguous-storage rewrite approaches.

novelty_classification.note: Corrects the MAGNITUDE of F-EVO-7's flop claim (direction/growth stand). Closes the 'analytic flops not wall-clock' caveat repeated across E5-E8. criterion = §10-pinned tool, already a dev-dep -> no new dependency, no §1 issue.

components_added_or_updated: JunctionTreeCholesky note: MEASURED 5.9x-255x is deployable, 18x-1270x flops is the ceiling
framework_impact.caveat_closed: E5-E8 repeated caveat 'flop win is analytic not wall-clock' -> MEASURED (criterion).

corrections.F-EVO-7: flop speedup 18x-1270x is the CEILING; MEASURED wall-clock (criterion) is 5.9x-255x, ~5x below, due to per-clique small-Vec allocation overhead. Direction+growth stand; magnitude corrected.

honest_scope: single machine (Mac arm64); absolute us machine-specific, ratios transfer
dense baseline is Gaussian elim (~2x flops of pure Cholesky) -> ratio is a mild over-estimate of the pure win
both solvers re-factorize from scratch each solve
contiguous-storage rewrite (would close the ~5x gap) NOT built
dependency_note: criterion 0.5 already a dev-dependency (pinned in tools.yaml as the §10 benchmark tool) -> adding a [[bench]] is NOT a new dependency / no §1 core change.
environment_note: Run on the Mac; kato15 NOT synced this session -- origin main ahead, kato15 fast-forwards on next pull.

next_experiment_proposal.name: contiguous-storage multifrontal (one arena, no per-clique Vec) -> close the ~5x flop-to-measured gap; dense-Cholesky baseline; higher-order (degree>=3) representability
